title: dactylis glomerata var. hispanica
intro: Dactylis glomerata var. hispanica, also known as Spanish cock's foot or cocksfoot, is a variety of the wild grass Dactylis glomerata. This perennial grass is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and as a diuretic. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Dactylis glomerata var. hispanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile plant for natural remedies.
